questionText: The normal rain water is slightly acidic and its $\mathrm{pH}$ value is $5.6$ because of which one of the following?
subject: Chemistry
solution:
Normally rain water has a pH of 5.6 due to presence of H+ ions formed by the reaction of rain water with carbon dioxide present in the atmosphere.

$$ \begin{aligned} & \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})+\mathrm{CO}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \leftrightharpoons \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3(\mathrm{aq}) \\\\ & \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3(\mathrm{aq}) \leftrightharpoons \mathrm{H}^{+}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{HCO}_3^{-}(\mathrm{aq}) \end{aligned} $$

questionText:
The INCORRECT statement(s) about heavy water is (are)

(A) used as a moderator in nuclear reactor

(B) obtained as a by-product in fertilizer industry

(C) used for the study of reaction mechanism

(D) has a higher dielectric constant than water

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
subject: Chemistry
solution:
Heavy water (D<sub>2</sub>O) is obtained as a by-product in
the fertilizer industry. It is used as a moderator in
nuclear reactors and for the study of the reaction
mechanisms. Its dielectric constant is lower than that
of H<sub>2</sub>O.

questionText: The water having more dissolved O<sub>2</sub> is :
subject: Chemistry
solution: On heating concentration of O<sub>2</sub> in water decreases. So boiling water and water at 80$$^\circ$$C having less O<sub>2</sub> concentration. Polluted water also having less O<sub>2</sub> concentration. So water at 4$$^\circ$$C having maximum O<sub>2</sub> concentration.

questionText: Water does not produce CO on reacting with :
subject: Chemistry
solution:
CO2 + H2O $$ \to $$ H2CO3

C + H2O(steam) $$ \to $$ CO + H2

CH4 + H2O $$ \to $$ CO + 3H2

C3H8 + H2O $$ \to $$ 3CO + H2

questionText:
Given below are two statements : one is labelled as Assertion (A) and the other is labelled as Reason (R).

Assertion (A) : Heavy water is used for the study of reaction mechanism.

Reason (R) : The rate of reaction for the cleavage of O - H bond is slower than that of O - D bond.

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
options: Both (A) and (R) are true but (R) is not the true explanation of (A).
Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the true explanation of (A).
subject: Chemistry
solution: D<sub>2</sub>O in used for the study of reaction mechanism. Rate of reaction for the cleavage of O-H bond > O-D bond.

questionText:
Statements about heavy water are given below :

A. Heavy water is used in exchange reactions for the study of reaction mechanisms.

B. Heavy water is prepared by exhaustive electrolysis of water.

(C) Heavy water has higher boiling point than ordinary water.

(D) Viscosity of H2O is greater than D2O.

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
subject: Chemistry
solution:
Viscosity of D2O is greater than H2O.

B.P. of D2O is greater than H2O.

questionText:
The equation that represents the water-gas shift
reaction is :
subject: Chemistry
solution:
(A) Water gas shift reaction
CO(g) + H2O(g) $$\mathrel{\mathop{\kern0pt\longrightarrow} \limits_{Catalyst}^{673K}} $$ CO2(g) + H2(g)

(B) Water gas is produced by this reaction
CH4(g) + H2O(g) $$\mathrel{\mathop{\kern0pt\longrightarrow} \limits_{Ni}^{1270K}} $$ CO(g) + 3H2(g)

(C) Water gas is produced by this reaction
C(s) + H2O(g) $$\buildrel {1270K} \over \longrightarrow $$ CO(g) + H2(g)

(D) Producer gas is produced by this reaction
2C(s) + O2(g) + 4N2(g) $$\buildrel {1273K} \over \longrightarrow $$ 2CO(g) + 4N2(g)

questionText: The synonym for water gas when used in the production of methanol is :
subject: Chemistry
solution:
H2O(g) + C $$ \to $$ CO + H2

The gaseous mixture CO + H2 which is produced when steam is passed over red hot coke and this mixture called water gas or synthesis gas or syn gas.

questionText:
The number of water molecule(s) not
coordinated to copper ion directly in
CuSO<sub>4</sub>.5H<sub>2</sub>O, is :
subject: Chemistry

questionText: Which one of the following statements about water is FALSE?
options: Water is oxidized to oxygen during photosynthesis
Water can act both as an acid and as a base.
There is extensive intramolecular hydrogen bonding in the condensed phase.
Ice formed by heavy water sinks in normal water
subject: Chemistry
solution: There is extensive inter-molecular hydrogen bonding in the condensed phase instead of intra-molecular  $$H$$-bonding.

questionText: Identify the incorrect statement regarding heavy water :
options:
It reacts with Al<sub>4</sub>C<sub>3</sub> to produce CD<sub>4</sub> and Al(OD)<sub>3</sub>
.
It is used as a coolant in nuclear reactors.

It reacts with CaC<sub>2</sub> to produce C<sub>2</sub>D<sub>2</sub> and Ca(OD)<sub>2</sub>
.
It reacts with SO<sub>3</sub> to form deuterated sulphuric acid (D<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub>).
subject: Chemistry
solution:
Heavy water is used as moderator in nuclear reactors
to control the speed of neutrons.
